title: Stellaria werchojanensis
intro: Stellaria werchojanensis, also known as Werchoiskan Starwort, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the Caryophyllaceae family. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as colds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Stellaria werchojanensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Stellaria werchojanensis is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in the production of ointments and salves.
taxonomy: Stellaria werchojanensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It belongs to the genus Stellaria, which contains about 100 species of plants. Stellaria werchojanensis is further classified under the order Caryophyllales and the class Magnoliopsida. This plant is native to the Russian Far East and shares characteristics with other flowering plants in the Caryophyllaceae family. The classification of Stellaria werchojanensis highlights its position within the larger plant kingdom.

side_effects: Stellaria werchojanensis, also known as Siberian starwort, is a plant that has been traditionally used in folk medicine, but its potential side effects are not well-documented. However, its close relatives in the Stellaria genus have been reported to cause gastrointestinal issues, such as nausea and diarrhea, in some individuals.
